rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF SONIA GUTIERREZ
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Granted per written order. NB: A disclaimer is not a voidable transfer by the beneficiary under the Uniform Voidable Transactions Act (Chapter 1 (commencing with Section 3439) of Title 2 of Part 2 of Division 4 of the Civil Code). [Probate Code Section 283.] As a result of such refusal ["qualified disclaimer"], the interest passes without any direction on the part of the person making the disclaimer. [26 U.S. Code Section 2518(b)(4).]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF OTIS R. DAMSLET
rulings.Calendar Matter: REPORT OF STATUS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/30/2024, as the following issues (e-mailed to counsel on 06/26/2024) remain unaddressed: 1) No proposed order has been received or lodged. 2) No DE-120 form is on file. A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(C)(6) must be attached to and served with the form at least 15 days before the hearing. 3) The petition must be amended and renoticed to pray for a specific period (e.g., months) for which the administration of this estate is sought to be continued. See LRSF 14.35(J)(2). 4) Has the petitioner taken any IAEA actions reportable under CRC 7.250(a)? 5) Each status report must include all allegations required by LRSF 14.35(J)(1), even if they may have been included in previous reports. 6) The decedent died more than 2 years ago, but no first interim account covering the 2-year period from the date of death has been filed. See LRSF 14.34(A). If the beneficiaries waived the first account, please file signed waivers. Otherwise, the Court may require the first account. See section 12201(c)(1). Note: An appearance may be required on 10/30/2024 for the Court to consider whether this petition should be taken off calendar, unless papers showing satisfactory progress to address the issues are filed by 10/23/2024.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF FUMIKO TAKAGI
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/5/24. (No earlier availability.) The deadline was 8/26/24. Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The "supplement" filed on 8/20/24 AMENDED the petition. There was $198,906.67 on hand as of 5/10/24. Proposed distribution now begins with $198,092.75. An amending pleading (regardless of its caption as a "supplement") requires the same notice as the pleading it amends. [CRC 7.53(a).] (2) Directing the Court to review bank statements is NOT a proper accounting. Probate Code Section 1062 requires DETAILED SCHEDULES to be filed to support the summary of account. New deadline is 10/25/24 to provide the assigned examiner courtesy copies of the amendment to the petition and proof of service of the amendment and any papers filed to address these issues. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (Issues should be addressed in a supplement or an amendment to a petition (or a completely amended petition) that stands on its own, not in response to issues posted in a tentative ruling or to "Examiner's Notes.")

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JEREMY THOMAS BROWN
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order To Transfer Decedents Accounts
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1) The petition requests an order directing Apple Inc., as custodian of the decedent's digital assets, to disclose the contents stored in the decedent's Apple ID accounts, presumably including the content of electronic communications sent or received by the decedent (as defined in Probate Code section 871(d)), even though there is no record evidencing the decedent's consent to disclosure of the content of electronic communications. Section 876 provides in relevant part that: "If a deceased user consented to or a court directs disclosure of the content of electronic communications of the user, the custodian shall disclose to the personal representative of the estate of the user the content of an electronic communication sent or received by the user if the personal representative gives to the custodian all of the following: --- (d) Unless the user provided direction using an online tool, a copy of the user's will, trust, power of attorney, or other record evidencing the user's consent to disclosure of the content of electronic communications." Section 877 provides in relevant part that: "Unless the user prohibited disclosure of digital assets or the court directs otherwise, a custodian shall disclose to the personal representative of the estate of a deceased user a catalogue of electronic communications sent or received by the user and digital assets, other than the content of electronic communications, of the user." See also section 871(c) defining "catalogue of electronic communications". The Court is to determine whether to allow disclosure of the content of electronic communications, or whether to only allow disclosure of a catalogue of electronic communications, without disclosure of the content thereof, subject to a renewed ex parte petition to be entertained by the Court if the scope of disclosure of the content is limited by the sender, recipient, date, and time identified by the catalogue, with an explanation as to why the content must be reviewed. 2) The petition requests an order "resolving, and denying, quashing, or vacating as moot, any request, legal process, motion, or court order currently directed to or against Apple seeking contents and data" (see the last paragraph 4 of the proposed order). The Court has seen this language in cases involving Google, but this petition requests no relief against Google. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: KLEMENS KOPEN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/16/2024 . The following issues to be cleared two weeks prior to the date: 1. Need proof of service of the notice to the missing person. Prob. Code sec. 12405. 2. Need proof of publication. Prob. Code sec. 8003(b); 12405 3. Need recent declaration of due diligent search. The declaration (Exhibit B) was executed in 2019 in another case, describing efforts in 2018 and 2019. Petition does not describe all efforts to locate the missing person since then. 4. Petition appears to pray for the time of death to be determined as 5 years from 2001. Then, the missing person's parents are the heirs. Since they are post-deceased, Petition Attachment #8 must set forth the personal rep of each parent's estate or the successor-in-interest if no personal rep has been necessarily appointed. They must be properly notified, too. 5. Need further allegations as to why this missing person's probate is necessary. The nature of the assets that the missing person owns despite being missing for more than 20 years. 6. Duties and Liabilities should have been signed on or after the petition is signed. 7. Proposed Order to be revised to set forth the suggested time of death. "Unknown" appears insufficient when the attachment seeks the court to find the date of death is five years from 2001. 8. Need clarification on whether the petitioner intends to withdraw one of the petitions that seek identical relief. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.
